run_betweenness_centrality.description: Dictionary keyed by node with bipartite betweenness centrality as the value.

run_betweenness_centrality_subset.description: Dictionary of nodes with betweenness centrality as the value.

run_closeness_centrality.description: Dictionary keyed by node with bipartite closeness centrality as the value.

run_current_flow_betweenness_centrality_.description: Dictionary of nodes with betweenness centrality as the value.

run_current_flow_closeness_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with current flow closeness centrality as the value.

run_degree_centrality.description: Dictionary keyed by node with bipartite degree centrality as the value.

run_dispersion.description: If u (v) is specified, returns a dictionary of nodes with dispersion score for all "target" ("source") nodes. If neither u nor v is specified, returns a dictionary of dictionaries for all nodes 'u' in the graph with a dispersion score for each node 'v'.

run_edge_betweenness_centrality.description: Dictionary of edges with betweenness centrality as the value.

run_edge_betweenness_centrality_subset.description: Dictionary of edges with Betweenness centrality as the value.

run_edge_current_flow_betweenness_centra.description: Dictionary of edge tuples with betweenness centrality as the value.
Dictionary of edge tuples with betweenness centrality as the value.

run_edge_load_centrality.description: A dict keyed by edge 2-tuple to the number of shortest paths which use that edge. Where more than one path is shortest the count is divided equally among paths.

run_eigenvector_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with eigenvector centrality as the value. The associated vector has unit Euclidean norm and the values are nonegative.

run_eigenvector_centrality_numpy.description: Dictionary of nodes with eigenvector centrality as the value. The associated vector has unit Euclidean norm and the values are nonnegative.

run_global_reaching_centrality.description: The global reaching centrality of the graph.

run_group_betweenness_centrality.description: If C is a single group then return a float. If C is a list with several groups then return a list of group betweenness centralities.

run_group_closeness_centrality.description: Group closeness centrality of the group S.

run_group_degree_centrality.description: Group degree centrality of the group S.

run_group_in_degree_centrality.description: Group in-degree centrality of the group S.

run_group_out_degree_centrality.description: Group out-degree centrality of the group S.

run_harmonic_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with harmonic centrality as the value.

run_in_degree_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with in-degree centrality as values.

run_incremental_closeness_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with closeness centrality as the value.

run_information_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with current flow closeness centrality as the value.

run_katz_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with Katz centrality as the value.

run_katz_centrality_numpy.description: Dictionary of nodes with Katz centrality as the value.

run_laplacian_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with Laplacian centrality as the value.

run_load_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with centrality as the value.

run_local_reaching_centrality.description: The local reaching centrality of the node ``v`` in the graph ``G``.

run_out_degree_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with out-degree centrality as values.

run_percolation_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with percolation centrality as the value.

run_prominent_group.description: The group betweenness centrality of the prominent group. max_group : list The list of nodes in the prominent group.

run_second_order_centrality.description: Dictionary keyed by node with second order centrality as the value.

run_subgraph_centrality.description: Dictionary of nodes with subgraph centrality as the value.

run_subgraph_centrality_exp.description: nodes:dictionary Dictionary of nodes with subgraph centrality as the value.

run_trophic_differences.description: Dictionary of edges with trophic differences as the value.

run_trophic_incoherence_parameter.description: The trophic coherence of a graph

run_trophic_levels.description: Dictionary of nodes with trophic level as the value.

run_voterank.description: Ordered list of computed seeds. Only nodes with positive number of votes are returned.

run_asyn_fluidc.description: Iterable of communities given as sets of nodes.

run_asyn_lpa_communities.description: Iterable of communities given as sets of nodes.

run_edge_betweenness_partition.description: Partition of the nodes of G

run_fast_label_propagation_communities.description: Iterable of communities given as sets of nodes.

run_girvan_newman.description: iterator Iterator over tuples of sets of nodes in `G`. Each set of node is a community, each tuple is a sequence of communities at a particular level of the algorithm.

run_greedy_modularity_communities.description: communities: list A list of frozensets of nodes, one for each community. Sorted by length with largest communities first.

run_greedy_source_expansion.description: set A set of nodes representing the local community around the source node.

run_k_clique_communities.description: Yields sets of nodes, one for each k-clique community.

run_kernighan_lin_bisection.description: A pair of sets of nodes representing the bipartition.

run_label_propagation_communities.description: A dict_values object that contains a set of nodes for each community.

run_leiden_communities.description: list A list of disjoint sets (partition of `G`). Each set represents one community. All communities together contain all the nodes in `G`.

run_louvain_communities.description: list A list of sets (partition of `G`). Each set represents one community and contains all the nodes that constitute it.

run_lukes_partitioning.description: A list of sets of nodes representing the clusters of the partition.

run_naive_greedy_modularity_communities.description: list A list of sets of nodes, one for each community. Sorted by length with largest communities first.

run_partition_quality.description: (float, float) The (coverage, performance) tuple of the partition, as defined above.

run_pagerank.description: Dictionary of nodes with PageRank as value

run_hits.description: Two dictionaries keyed by node containing the hub and authority values.

run_attracting_components.description: A generator of sets of nodes, one for each attracting component of G.

run_biconnected_component_edges.description: Generator of lists of edges, one list for each bicomponent.

run_biconnected_components.description: Generator of sets of nodes, one set for each biconnected component.

run_condensation.description: The condensation graph C of G.  The node labels are integers corresponding to the index of the component in the list of strongly connected components of G.  C has a graph attribute named 'mapping' with a dictionary mapping the original nodes to the nodes in C to which they belong.  Each node in C also has a node attribute 'members' with the set of original nodes in G that form the SCC that the node in C represents.

run_connected_components.description: A generator of sets of nodes, one for each component of G.

run_is_attracting_component.description: True if `G` has a single attracting component. Otherwise, False.

run_is_biconnected.description: True if the graph is biconnected, False otherwise.

run_is_connected.description: True if the graph is connected, false otherwise.

run_is_semiconnected.description: True if the graph is semiconnected, False otherwise.

run_is_strongly_connected.description: bool Whether the tournament is strongly connected.

run_is_weakly_connected.description: True if the graph is weakly connected, False otherwise.

run_kosaraju_strongly_connected_componen.description: A generator of sets of nodes, one for each strongly connected component of G.

run_node_connected_component.description: A set of nodes in the component of G containing node n.

run_number_attracting_components.description: The number of attracting components in G.

run_strongly_connected_components.description: A generator of sets of nodes, one for each strongly connected component of G.

run_weakly_connected_components.description: A generator of sets of nodes, one for each weakly connected component of G.

run_all_node_cuts.description: Each node cutset has cardinality equal to the node connectivity of the input graph.

run_all_pairs_node_connectivity.description: A dictionary with node connectivity between all pairs of nodes in G, or in nbunch if provided.

run_edge_connectivity.description: Edge connectivity for G, or local edge connectivity if source and target were provided

run_edge_disjoint_paths.description: A generator of edge independent paths.

run_is_k_edge_connected.description: boolean True if G is k-edge-connected.

run_is_locally_k_edge_connected.description: boolean True if s and t are locally k-edge-connected in G.

run_k_components.description: Dictionary with all connectivity levels `k` in the input Graph as keys and a list of sets of nodes that form a k-component of level `k` as values.

run_k_edge_components.type: a generator of k-edge-ccs. Each set of returned nodes
run_k_edge_components.description: will have k-edge-connectivity in the graph G.

run_k_edge_subgraphs.description: Each k-edge-subgraph is a maximal set of nodes that defines a subgraph of G that is k-edge-connected.

run_local_edge_connectivity.description: local edge connectivity for nodes s and t.

run_local_node_connectivity.description: local node connectivity for nodes s and t

run_minimum_edge_cut.description: Set of edges that, if removed, would disconnect G. If source and target nodes are provided, the set contains the edges that if removed, would destroy all paths between source and target.

run_minimum_node_cut.description: Set of nodes that, if removed, would disconnect G. If source and target nodes are provided, the set contains the nodes that if removed, would destroy all paths between source and target.

run_minimum_st_edge_cut.description: Set of edges that, if removed from the graph, will disconnect it.

run_minimum_st_node_cut.description: Set of nodes that, if removed, would destroy all paths between source and target in G. Returns an empty set if source and target are either in different components or are directly connected by an edge, as no node removal can destroy the path.

run_node_connectivity.description: Node connectivity of G, or local node connectivity if source and target are provided.

run_stoer_wagner.description: The sum of weights of edges in a minimum cut. partition : pair of node lists A partitioning of the nodes that defines a minimum cut.

run_all_pairs_all_shortest_paths.description: Dictionary of arrays, keyed by source and target, of all shortest paths.

run_shortest_path.description: path: list or dictionary or iterator All returned paths include both the source and target in the path. If the source and target are both specified, return a single list of nodes in a shortest path from the source to the target. If only the source is specified, return a dictionary keyed by targets with a list of nodes in a shortest path from the source to one of the targets. If only the target is specified, return a dictionary keyed by sources with a list of nodes in a shortest path from one of the sources to the target. If neither the source nor target are specified, return an iterator over (source, dictionary) where dictionary is keyed by target to list of nodes in a shortest path from the source to the target.

run_single_source_all_shortest_paths.description: A generator of all paths between source and all nodes in the graph.

run_all_pairs_bellman_ford_path.description: (source, dictionary) iterator with dictionary keyed by target and shortest path as the key value.

run_all_pairs_bellman_ford_path_length.description: (source, dictionary) iterator with dictionary keyed by target and shortest path length as the key value.

run_all_pairs_dijkstra_path.description: (source, dictionary) iterator with dictionary keyed by target and shortest path as the key value.

run_all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length.description: (source, dictionary) iterator with dictionary keyed by target and shortest path length as the key value.

run_bellman_ford_path.description: List of nodes in a shortest path.

run_bellman_ford_predecessor_and_distanc.description: Returns two dictionaries keyed by node to predecessor in the path and to the distance from the source respectively.

run_bidirectional_dijkstra.description: length is the distance from source to target. path is a list of nodes on a path from source to target.

run_dijkstra_path.description: List of nodes in a shortest path.

run_dijkstra_predecessor_and_distance.description: Returns two dictionaries representing a list of predecessors of a node and the distance to each node.

run_find_negative_cycle.description: A list of nodes in the order of the cycle found. The last node equals the first to indicate a cycle.

run_goldberg_radzik.description: Returns two dictionaries keyed by node to predecessor in the path and to the distance from the source respectively.

run_johnson.description: Dictionary, keyed by source and target, of shortest paths.

run_multi_source_dijkstra.type: pair of dictionaries, or numeric and list
run_multi_source_dijkstra.description: If target is None, returns a tuple of two dictionaries keyed by node. The first dictionary stores distance from one of the source nodes. The second stores the path from one of the sources to that node. If target is not None, returns a tuple of (distance, path) where distance is the distance from source to target and path is a list representing the path from source to target.

run_multi_source_dijkstra_path.description: Dictionary of shortest paths keyed by target.

run_multi_source_dijkstra_path_length.description: Dict keyed by node to shortest path length to nearest source.

run_negative_edge_cycle.description: True if a negative edge cycle exists, otherwise False.

run_single_source_bellman_ford.type: pair of dictionaries, or numeric and list
run_single_source_bellman_ford.description: If target is None, returns a tuple of two dictionaries keyed by node. The first dictionary stores distance from one of the source nodes. The second stores the path from one of the sources to that node. If target is not None, returns a tuple of (distance, path) where distance is the distance from source to target and path is a list representing the path from source to target.

run_single_source_bellman_ford_path.description: Dictionary of shortest path lengths keyed by target.

run_single_source_bellman_ford_path_leng.description: Dictionary of shortest path length keyed by target

run_single_source_dijkstra.type: pair of dictionaries, or numeric and list.
run_single_source_dijkstra.description: If target is None, paths and lengths to all nodes are computed. The return value is a tuple of two dictionaries keyed by target nodes. The first dictionary stores distance to each target node. The second stores the path to each target node. If target is not None, returns a tuple (distance, path), where distance is the distance from source to target and path is a list representing the path from source to target.

run_single_source_dijkstra_path.description: Dictionary of shortest path lengths keyed by target.

run_single_source_dijkstra_path_length.description: Dict keyed by node to shortest path length from source.

run_all_pairs_shortest_path.description: Dictionary, keyed by source and target, of shortest paths.

run_all_pairs_shortest_path_length.description: (source, dictionary) iterator with dictionary keyed by target and shortest path length as the key value.

run_bidirectional_shortest_path.description: path: list List of nodes in a path from source to target.

run_predecessor.description: Dictionary, keyed by node, of predecessors in the shortest path. (pred, seen): tuple of dictionaries If `return_seen` argument is set to `True`, then a tuple of dictionaries is returned. The first element is the dictionary, keyed by node, of predecessors in the shortest path. The second element is the dictionary, keyed by node, of the level (number of hops) to reach the node (as seen during breadth-first-search).

run_single_source_shortest_path.description: Dictionary, keyed by target, of shortest paths.

run_single_source_shortest_path_length.description: Dict keyed by node to shortest path length to `source`.

run_single_target_shortest_path.description: Dictionary, keyed by target, of shortest paths.

run_single_target_shortest_path_length.description: Dictionary, keyed by source, of shortest path lengths.

run_floyd_warshall.description: A dictionary,  keyed by source and target, of shortest paths distances between nodes.

run_floyd_warshall_numpy.description: A numpy array of shortest path distances between nodes. If there is no path between two nodes the value is Inf.

run_floyd_warshall_predecessor_and_dista.description: Dictionaries, keyed by source and target, of predecessors and distances in the shortest path.

run_is_simple_path.description: bool Whether the given list of nodes represents a simple path in `G`.

run_shortest_simple_paths.description: path_generator: generator A generator that produces lists of simple paths, in order from shortest to longest.

run_average_clustering.description: The average bipartite clustering for the given set of nodes or the entire graph if no nodes are specified.

run_clustering.description: A dictionary keyed by node with the clustering coefficient value.

run_generalized_degree.description: Generalized degree of specified nodes. The Counter is keyed by edge triangle multiplicity.

run_square_clustering.description: A dictionary keyed by node with the square clustering coefficient value.

run_triangles.description: If `nodes` is a container of nodes, returns number of triangles keyed by node (dict). If `nodes` is a specific node, returns number of triangles for the node (int).

run_enumerate_all_cliques.description: iterator An iterator over cliques, each of which is a list of nodes in `G`. The cliques are ordered according to size.

run_find_cliques.description: iterator An iterator over maximal cliques, each of which is a list of nodes in `G`. If `nodes` is provided, only the maximal cliques containing all the nodes in `nodes` are returned. The order of cliques is arbitrary.

run_find_cliques_recursive.description: iterator An iterator over maximal cliques, each of which is a list of nodes in `G`. If `nodes` is provided, only the maximal cliques containing all the nodes in `nodes` are yielded. The order of cliques is arbitrary.

run_make_clique_bipartite.description: NetworkX graph A bipartite graph whose "bottom" set is the nodes of the graph `G`, whose "top" set is the cliques of `G`, and whose edges join nodes of `G` to the cliques that contain them. The nodes of the graph `G` have the node attribute 'bipartite' set to 1 and the nodes representing cliques have the node attribute 'bipartite' set to 0, as is the convention for bipartite graphs in NetworkX.

run_make_max_clique_graph.description: NetworkX graph A graph whose nodes are the cliques of `G` and whose edges join two cliques if they are not disjoint.

run_max_weight_clique.description: the nodes of a maximum weight clique weight : int the weight of a maximum weight clique

run_node_clique_number.description: int or dict If `nodes` is a single node, returns the size of the largest maximal clique in `G` containing that node. Otherwise return a dict keyed by node to the size of the largest maximal clique containing that node.

run_core_number.description: A dictionary keyed by node to the core number.

run_onion_layers.description: A dictionary keyed by node to the onion layer. The layers are contiguous integers starting at 1.

run_barycenter.description: list Nodes of `G` that induce the barycenter of `G`.

run_eccentricity.description: A dictionary of eccentricity values keyed by node.

run_kemeny_constant.description: float The Kemeny constant of the graph `G`.

run_resistance_distance.description: If `nodeA` and `nodeB` are given, resistance distance between `nodeA` and `nodeB`. If `nodeA` or `nodeB` is unspecified (the default), a dictionary of nodes with resistance distances as the value.

run_is_matching.description: bool Whether the given set or dictionary represents a valid matching in the graph.

run_is_maximal_matching.description: bool Whether the given set or dictionary represents a valid maximal matching in the graph.

run_is_perfect_matching.description: bool Whether the given set or dictionary represents a valid perfect matching in the graph.

run_max_weight_matching.description: A maximal matching of the graph.

run_maximal_matching.description: A maximal matching of the graph.

run_min_weight_matching.description: A minimal weight matching of the graph.

run_boykov_kolmogorov.description: Residual network after computing the maximum flow.

run_capacity_scaling.description: Cost of a minimum cost flow satisfying all demands. flowDict : dictionary If G is a digraph, a dict-of-dicts keyed by nodes such that flowDict[u][v] is the flow on edge (u, v). If G is a MultiDiGraph, a dict-of-dicts-of-dicts keyed by nodes so that flowDict[u][v][key] is the flow on edge (u, v, key).

run_cost_of_flow.description: The total cost of the flow. This is given by the sum over all edges of the product of the edge's flow and the edge's weight.

run_dinitz.description: Residual network after computing the maximum flow.

run_edmonds_karp.description: Residual network after computing the maximum flow.

run_gomory_hu_tree.description: A NetworkX graph representing the Gomory-Hu tree of the input graph.

run_max_flow_min_cost.description: flowDict: dictionary Dictionary of dictionaries keyed by nodes such that flowDict[u][v] is the flow edge (u, v).

run_min_cost_flow.description: Dictionary of dictionaries keyed by nodes such that flowDict[u][v] is the flow edge (u, v).

run_min_cost_flow_cost.description: Cost of a minimum cost flow satisfying all demands.

run_network_simplex.description: Cost of a minimum cost flow satisfying all demands. flowDict : dictionary Dictionary of dictionaries keyed by nodes such that flowDict[u][v] is the flow edge (u, v).

run_preflow_push.description: Residual network after computing the maximum flow.

run_shortest_augmenting_path.description: Residual network after computing the maximum flow.

run_is_arborescence.description: A boolean that is True if `G` is an arborescence.

run_is_branching.description: A boolean that is True if `G` is a branching.

run_is_forest.description: A boolean that is True if `G` is a forest.

run_is_tree.description: A boolean that is True if `G` is a tree.

run_maximum_spanning_edges.description: An iterator over edges in a maximum spanning tree of `G`. Edges connecting nodes `u` and `v` are represented as tuples: `(u, v, k, d)` or `(u, v, k)` or `(u, v, d)` or `(u, v)` If `G` is a multigraph, `keys` indicates whether the edge key `k` will be reported in the third position in the edge tuple. `data` indicates whether the edge datadict `d` will appear at the end of the edge tuple. If `G` is not a multigraph, the tuples are `(u, v, d)` if `data` is True or `(u, v)` if `data` is False.

run_maximum_spanning_tree.description: A maximum spanning tree or forest.

run_minimum_spanning_edges.description: An iterator over edges in a maximum spanning tree of `G`. Edges connecting nodes `u` and `v` are represented as tuples: `(u, v, k, d)` or `(u, v, k)` or `(u, v, d)` or `(u, v)` If `G` is a multigraph, `keys` indicates whether the edge key `k` will be reported in the third position in the edge tuple. `data` indicates whether the edge datadict `d` will appear at the end of the edge tuple. If `G` is not a multigraph, the tuples are `(u, v, d)` if `data` is True or `(u, v)` if `data` is False.

run_minimum_spanning_tree.description: A minimum spanning tree or forest.

run_number_of_spanning_trees.description: Number Undirected graphs: The number of spanning trees of the graph `G`. Or the sum of all spanning tree weights of the graph `G` where the weight of a tree is the product of its edge weights. Directed graphs: The number of arborescences of `G` rooted at node `root`. Or the sum of all arborescence weights of the graph `G` with specified root where the weight of an arborescence is the product of its edge weights.

run_partition_spanning_tree.description: A minimum spanning tree using all of the included edges in the graph and none of the excluded edges.

run_random_spanning_tree.description: nx.Graph A spanning tree using the distribution defined by the weight of the tree.

run_dag_to_branching.description: DiGraph The branching in which there is a bijection between root-to-leaf paths in `G` (in which multiple paths may share the same leaf) and root-to-leaf paths in the branching (in which there is a unique path from a root to a leaf). Each node has an attribute 'source' whose value is the original node to which this node corresponds. No other graph, node, or edge attributes are copied into this new graph.

run_is_aperiodic.description: bool True if the graph is aperiodic False otherwise

run_is_directed_acyclic_graph.description: bool True if `G` is a DAG, False otherwise

run_transitive_closure.description: NetworkX graph The transitive closure of `G`

run_transitive_closure_dag.description: NetworkX DiGraph The transitive closure of `G`

run_transitive_reduction.description: NetworkX DiGraph The transitive reduction of `G`

run_cycle_basis.description: A list of cycle lists.  Each cycle list is a list of nodes which forms a cycle (loop) in G.

run_find_cycle.description: A list of directed edges indicating the path taken for the loop. If no cycle is found, then an exception is raised. For graphs, an edge is of the form `(u, v)` where `u` and `v` are the tail and head of the edge as determined by the traversal. For multigraphs, an edge is of the form `(u, v, key)`, where `key` is the key of the edge. When the graph is directed, then `u` and `v` are always in the order of the actual directed edge. If orientation is not None then the edge tuple is extended to include the direction of traversal ('forward' or 'reverse') on that edge.

run_minimum_cycle_basis.description: A list of cycle lists.  Each cycle list is a list of nodes which forms a cycle (loop) in G. Note that the nodes are not necessarily returned in a order by which they appear in the cycle

run_recursive_simple_cycles.description: A list of cycles, where each cycle is represented by a list of nodes along the cycle. Example: >>> edges = [(0, 0), (0, 1), (0, 2), (1, 2), (2, 0), (2, 1), (2, 2)] >>> G = nx.DiGraph(edges) >>> nx.recursive_simple_cycles(G) [[0], [2], [0, 1, 2], [0, 2], [1, 2]]

run_equitable_color.description: A dictionary with keys representing nodes and values representing corresponding coloring.

run_greedy_color.description: A dictionary with keys representing nodes and values representing corresponding coloring.

run_generate_random_paths.description: Generator of `sample_size` paths each with length `path_length`.

run_panther_similarity.description: Dictionary of nodes to similarity scores (as floats). Note: the self-similarity (i.e., ``v``) will not be included in the returned dictionary. So, for ``k = 5``, a dictionary of top 4 nodes and their similarity scores will be returned.

run_simrank_similarity.description: If ``source`` and ``target`` are both ``None``, this returns a dictionary of dictionaries, where keys are node pairs and value are similarity of the pair of nodes. If ``source`` is not ``None`` but ``target`` is, this returns a dictionary mapping node to the similarity of ``source`` and that node. If neither ``source`` nor ``target`` is ``None``, this returns the similarity value for the given pair of nodes.

run_adamic_adar_index.description: An iterator of 3-tuples in the form (u, v, p) where (u, v) is a pair of nodes and p is their Adamic-Adar index.

run_cn_soundarajan_hopcroft.description: An iterator of 3-tuples in the form (u, v, p) where (u, v) is a pair of nodes and p is their score.

run_common_neighbor_centrality.description: An iterator of 3-tuples in the form (u, v, p) where (u, v) is a pair of nodes and p is their Common Neighbor and Centrality based Parameterized Algorithm(CCPA) score.

run_jaccard_coefficient.description: An iterator of 3-tuples in the form (u, v, p) where (u, v) is a pair of nodes and p is their Jaccard coefficient.

run_preferential_attachment.description: An iterator of 3-tuples in the form (u, v, p) where (u, v) is a pair of nodes and p is their preferential attachment score.

run_ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft.description: An iterator of 3-tuples in the form (u, v, p) where (u, v) is a pair of nodes and p is their score.

run_resource_allocation_index.description: An iterator of 3-tuples in the form (u, v, p) where (u, v) is a pair of nodes and p is their resource allocation index.

run_within_inter_cluster.description: An iterator of 3-tuples in the form (u, v, p) where (u, v) is a pair of nodes and p is their WIC measure.

run_biadjacency_matrix.description: Biadjacency matrix representation of the bipartite graph G.

run_birank.description: Dictionary keyed by node to that node's BiRank score.

run_color.description: A dictionary keyed by node with a 1 or 0 as data for each node color.

run_eppstein_matching.description: The matching is returned as a dictionary, `matching`, such that ``matching[v] == w`` if node `v` is matched to node `w`. Unmatched nodes do not occur as a key in `matching`.

run_generate_edgelist.description: Lines of data in adjlist format.

run_hopcroft_karp_matching.description: The matching is returned as a dictionary, `matches`, such that ``matches[v] == w`` if node `v` is matched to node `w`. Unmatched nodes do not occur as a key in `matches`.

run_latapy_clustering.description: A dictionary keyed by node with the clustering coefficient value.

run_maximum_matching.description: The matching is returned as a dictionary, `matches`, such that ``matches[v] == w`` if node `v` is matched to node `w`. Unmatched nodes do not occur as a key in `matches`.

run_min_edge_cover.description: set A set of the edges in a minimum edge cover of the graph, given as pairs of nodes. It contains both the edges `(u, v)` and `(v, u)` for given nodes `u` and `v` among the edges of minimum edge cover.

run_minimum_weight_full_matching.description: The matching is returned as a dictionary, `matches`, such that ``matches[v] == w`` if node `v` is matched to node `w`. Unmatched nodes do not occur as a key in `matches`.

run_node_redundancy.description: A dictionary keyed by node with the node redundancy value.

run_robins_alexander_clustering.description: The Robins and Alexander bipartite clustering for the input graph.

run_sets.description: Nodes from one side of the bipartite graph. Y : set Nodes from the other side.

run_spectral_bipartivity.description: A single number if the keyword nodes is not specified, or a dictionary keyed by node with the spectral bipartivity contribution of that node as the value.

run_bfs_predecessors.description: pred: iterator (node, predecessor) iterator where `predecessor` is the predecessor of `node` in a breadth first search starting from `source`.

run_bfs_successors.description: succ: iterator (node, successors) iterator where `successors` is the non-empty list of successors of `node` in a breadth first search from `source`. To appear in the iterator, `node` must have successors.

run_bfs_tree.description: T: NetworkX DiGraph An oriented tree

run_descendants_at_distance.description: set() The descendants of `source` in `G` at the given `distance` from `source`

run_dfs_labeled_edges.description: edges: generator A generator of triples of the form (*u*, *v*, *d*), where (*u*, *v*) is the edge being explored in the depth-first search and *d* is one of the strings 'forward', 'nontree', 'reverse', or 'reverse-depth_limit'. A 'forward' edge is one in which *u* has been visited but *v* has not. A 'nontree' edge is one in which both *u* and *v* have been visited but the edge is not in the DFS tree. A 'reverse' edge is one in which both *u* and *v* have been visited and the edge is in the DFS tree. When the `depth_limit` is reached via a 'forward' edge, a 'reverse' edge is immediately generated rather than the subtree being explored. To indicate this flavor of 'reverse' edge, the string yielded is 'reverse-depth_limit'.

run_dfs_postorder_nodes.description: nodes: generator A generator of nodes in a depth-first-search post-ordering.

run_dfs_predecessors.description: pred: dict A dictionary with nodes as keys and predecessor nodes as values.

run_dfs_preorder_nodes.description: nodes: generator A generator of nodes in a depth-first-search pre-ordering.

run_dfs_successors.description: succ: dict A dictionary with nodes as keys and list of successor nodes as values.

run_hamiltonian_path.description: A list of nodes which form a Hamiltonian path in `G`.

run_is_path.description: bool Whether the given list of nodes represents a simple path in `G`.

run_is_reachable.description: bool Whether there is a path from `s` to `t` in `G`.

run_is_tournament.description: bool Whether the given graph is a tournament graph.

run_score_sequence.description: list A sorted list of the out-degrees of the nodes of `G`.

run_tournament_matrix.description: SciPy sparse array The tournament matrix of the tournament graph `G`.

run_cartesian_product.description: P: NetworkX graph The Cartesian product of G and H. P will be a multi-graph if either G or H is a multi-graph. Will be a directed if G and H are directed, and undirected if G and H are undirected.

run_compose.description: C: A new graph with the same type as G

run_corona_product.description: C: NetworkX graph The Corona product of G and H.

run_difference.type: A new graph with the same type as G.
run_difference.description: A new graph with the same type as G.

run_disjoint_union.type: A union graph with the same type as G.
run_disjoint_union.description: A union graph with the same type as G.

run_full_join.type: The full join graph with the same type as G.
run_full_join.description: The full join graph with the same type as G.

run_intersection.type: A new graph with the same type as G.
run_intersection.description: A new graph with the same type as G.

run_lexicographic_product.description: P: NetworkX graph The Cartesian product of G and H. P will be a multi-graph if either G or H is a multi-graph. Will be a directed if G and H are directed, and undirected if G and H are undirected.

run_modular_product.description: M: NetworkX graph The Modular product of `G` and `H`.

run_power.description: NetworkX simple graph `G` to the power `k`.

run_rooted_product.type: The rooted product of G and H with a specified root in H
run_rooted_product.description: The rooted product of G and H with a specified root in H

run_strong_product.description: P: NetworkX graph The Cartesian product of G and H. P will be a multi-graph if either G or H is a multi-graph. Will be a directed if G and H are directed, and undirected if G and H are undirected.

run_symmetric_difference.type: A new graph with the same type as G.
run_symmetric_difference.description: A new graph with the same type as G.

run_tensor_product.description: P: NetworkX graph The tensor product of G and H. P will be a multi-graph if either G or H is a multi-graph, will be a directed if G and H are directed, and undirected if G and H are undirected.

run_union.type: A union graph with the same type as G.
run_union.description: A union graph with the same type as G.

run_connected_dominating_set.description: A dominating set of nodes which induces a connected subgraph of G.

run_is_connected_dominating_set.description: True if `nbunch` is connected dominating set of `G`, false otherwise.

run_is_dominating_set.description: True if `nbunch` is a dominating set of `G`, false otherwise.

run_efficiency.description: float Multiplicative inverse of the shortest path distance between the nodes.

run_global_efficiency.description: float The average global efficiency of the graph.

run_local_efficiency.description: float The average local efficiency of the graph.

run_eulerian_circuit.description: An iterator over edges in the Eulerian circuit.

run_has_eulerian_path.type: True if G has an Eulerian path.
run_has_eulerian_path.description: True if G has an Eulerian path.

run_attribute_assortativity_coefficient.description: r: float Assortativity of graph for given attribute

run_attribute_mixing_dict.description: Counts or joint probability of occurrence of attribute pairs.

run_attribute_mixing_matrix.description: m: numpy array Counts or joint probability of occurrence of attribute pairs.

run_average_degree_connectivity.description: A dictionary keyed by degree k with the value of average connectivity.

run_average_neighbor_degree.description: d: dict A dictionary keyed by node to the average degree of its neighbors.

run_degree_mixing_dict.description: d: dictionary Counts or joint probability of occurrence of degree pairs.

run_degree_mixing_matrix.description: m: numpy array Counts, or joint probability, of occurrence of node degree.

run_numeric_assortativity_coefficient.description: r: float Assortativity of graph for given attribute

run_closeness_vitality.description: dictionary or float If `node` is None, this function returns a dictionary with nodes as keys and closeness vitality as the value. Otherwise, it returns only the closeness vitality for the specified `node`. The closeness vitality of a node may be negative infinity if removing that node would disconnect the graph.

run_gutman_index.description: number The Gutman Index of the graph `G`.

run_schultz_index.description: number The first kind of Schultz Index of the graph `G`.

run_wiener_index.description: number The Wiener index of the graph `G`.
